name: Railroad Coleslaw
directions: In large bowl place cabbage, onion and green pepper; toss to combine.
Sprinkle the sugar over the top.
Bring remaining ingredients to a boil; simmer 2 minutes.
Pour over cabbage mixture.
Cover bowl and refrigerate overnight.
Toss before serving.

name: Oriental Broccoli Slaw
directions: Mix olive oil, vinegar, sugar and packets of seasonings from Ramen noodles.
Let sit for 15 minutes.
In a skillet toast almonds in a little butter and set aside to cool.
Chop green onions and other vegetables, of your choice.
Mix vegetables with Ramen noodles.
Add broccoli slaw to noodles and vegetables.
Stir in sauce mix and almonds.
Marinate with sauce for about 1 hour.
The longer it sits the more limp the noodles get.
If you want the noodles to be more crunchy eat it sooner.

name: Shortcut Coleslaw
directions: Mix soup, mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, mustard, celery seed and pepper in a large bowl.
Toss with shredded cabbage and carrots until evenly coated.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours.

name: Crispy Bacon Coleslaw
directions: Mix dressing and sugar in large bowl.
Add remaining ingredients; mix lightly.
Refrigerate.

name: Best Low Carb Coleslaw
directions: In a large bowl, combine the coleslaw and onion.
Put aside.
In a separate bowl, combine the Splenda, salt, pepper, celery seed, mayonnaise,whipping cream, vinegar and lemon juice, using a whisk.
Pour over the coleslaw mix, stir gently to mix.
Refrigerate for 1 hour.

name: Red Cabbage Coleslaw
directions: Combine vegetables in a large mixing bowl.
In a microwavable bowl combine oil, vinegar, and sugar;  Microwave uncovered for about 45 seconds to dissolve sugar - NOTE if you are using splenda omit this step.
Pour hot dressing over vegetables; mix well.
Refrigerate to blend flavours.
Leftovers will keep a couple days in the fridge.

name: Copycat Kfc Cole Slaw
directions: Combine cabbage, carrots, sugar, salt and pepper.
Pour milk over and chill for 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix remaining ingredients.
Once cabbage mixture is chilled, combine with mayo mixture.
Chill in fridge until ready to serve.

name: My Own Asian Cole Slaw
directions: Combine the cabbage, onion and Jalapeno in a bowl.
Stir together the dressing ingredients until well mixed.  I find the easiest way to do this is with a whisk.
Pour dressing over cabbage mixture, stir, cover, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Stuart Anderson's Black Angus Coleslaw
directions: Combine water, vinegar and sugar until sugar is dissolved.
Add cabbage to the mixture.
Toss thoroughly.
Marinate for at least an hour.
Drain thoroughly in a colander but save marinade to use in one more batch of coleslaw, if desired. (After two uses, the marinade loses its flavor.).
To make the dressing: In a bowl, combine the mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, sour cream, horseradish and paprika.
Toss drained cabbage with some of the dressing.
Serve on plates lined with lettuce leaves.

name: Kentucky Coleslaw from Gloria Pitzer
directions: Stir together coleslaw mixture, milk and sugar in a bowl.
Cover & refrigerate one hour.
In a separate bowl, thoroughly mix dressing ingredients together.
Stir dressing into the undrained coleslaw mixture.
Cover & refrigerate one hour or more before serving.
